scorecardDescription: Answer 12 quick questions to find out if you're losing money to avoidable taxes in retirement.
bookingCtaText: Schedule Your Free Tax Leak Review

questions.text: Do you have a clear plan for the order in which you'll draw from your various retirement accounts (401k, IRA, Roth, brokerage)?

options.text: Yes, I follow a specific, tax-optimized withdrawal sequence
I have a general idea but haven't formalized a strategy
I mostly withdraw based on immediate need
I'm not sure what the most tax-efficient approach would be

questions.text: Are you aware of how different income sources (pensions, Social Security, IRA withdrawals, investment gains) interact to affect your total tax bill?

options.text: Yes, I actively manage the mix to minimize taxes
I understand the basics but don't actively manage it
I know some income is taxed differently but haven't looked into it deeply
I haven't really thought about how they interact

questions.text: Have you evaluated whether taking some income earlier in retirement (before Social Security or RMDs begin) could reduce your lifetime tax burden?

options.text: Yes, I've modeled different scenarios with a professional
I've thought about it but haven't run the numbers
I've heard this might help but haven't explored it
No, I plan to defer income as long as possible

questions.text: Have you analyzed how the timing of your Social Security benefits affects the taxation of those benefits?

options.text: Yes, I've optimized my claiming strategy with taxes in mind
I know timing matters but haven't done a detailed analysis
I plan to claim based on age preference, not tax impact
I didn't know Social Security timing affected my taxes

questions.text: Do you know what percentage of your Social Security benefits will be taxable based on your combined income?

options.text: Yes, and I'm managing other income to minimize the taxable portion
I know some of it is taxable but not the exact percentage
I assume most or all of it will be taxed
I didn't know Social Security benefits could be taxed

questions.text: Have you explored whether converting some traditional IRA or 401(k) funds to a Roth IRA could save you money in the long run?

options.text: Yes, I do strategic Roth conversions based on tax bracket analysis
I've looked into it but haven't pulled the trigger
I've heard about Roth conversions but don't understand the benefit
No, I haven't considered this

questions.text: Are you aware of the 'tax bracket gap' — years when your income is lower (e.g., between retirement and Social Security/RMDs) that create a window for low-cost Roth conversions?

options.text: Yes, I'm actively using this window for conversions
I've heard about this but haven't acted on it
I'm not sure when my income will be lower
This is the first I'm hearing about this

questions.text: Do you have a plan in place to manage Required Minimum Distributions (RMDs) so they don't push you into a higher tax bracket?

options.text: Yes, I've been proactively reducing my pre-tax balance before RMDs start
I know RMDs are coming but haven't planned for the tax impact
I'll deal with RMDs when I have to
I'm not sure what RMDs are or when they start

questions.text: Have you calculated how your RMDs (combined with other income) could affect your Medicare premiums through IRMAA surcharges?

options.text: Yes, I factor IRMAA thresholds into my withdrawal planning
I know higher income can increase Medicare costs but haven't planned for it
I've heard of IRMAA but don't know how it applies to me
I didn't know retirement income could affect Medicare premiums

questions.text: Are you strategically managing capital gains in your taxable investment accounts — for example, harvesting losses or timing sales to stay in lower brackets?

options.text: Yes, I actively manage gains and losses for tax efficiency
I try to be mindful but don't have a formal strategy
I sell investments when I need the money without considering tax impact
I haven't thought about capital gains management

questions.text: Is your investment portfolio structured with tax efficiency in mind — for example, holding tax-inefficient assets (bonds, REITs) in tax-advantaged accounts?

options.text: Yes, my portfolio uses asset location strategies
I have some awareness but haven't optimized placement
My investments are spread without much thought to tax efficiency
I'm not sure what asset location means

questions.text: Do you work with a financial advisor or tax professional who creates a multi-year tax projection for your retirement?

options.text: Yes, I have a comprehensive, forward-looking tax plan reviewed annually
My advisor addresses taxes but we don't do multi-year projections
I rely on my tax preparer at filing time but don't plan ahead
I don't currently work with anyone on retirement tax planning

tiers.label: Low Tax Leak
tiers.message: Your tax strategy looks solid. A quick review could confirm you're not leaving anything on the table.

tiers.label: Moderate Tax Leak
tiers.message: There may be tax-saving opportunities you're not taking advantage of. A conversation could uncover them.

tiers.label: High Tax Leak
tiers.message: You could be losing significant money to avoidable taxes. A professional review is strongly recommended.
